summary: Comprehensive E2E backend testing of ASTRIX AI completed against public preview URL. All 21 tests passed (100%) covering health, auth enforcement, Supabase RPC workspace creation, sample seeder, full AI fallback chain (classify/memo/proof/ask), opportunity scoring (single + bulk), Dodo LIVE billing checkout, pricing & status, team invite + accept flow, RLS workspace isolation, and AI quota RPC. Real Dodo LIVE checkout URLs were generated (no actual payment made).

minor.issue: Backend always sets role from invitation; doesn't validate role enum (member/admin/owner) on inbound invite payload server-side. Low risk because UI controls input, but recommend Pydantic enum.

Not directly tested (requires Dodo to fire). Code path looks correct; standard-webhooks signature verification is gated on all 3 headers being present — if any header is missing the webhook is accepted UNVERIFIED. Recommend rejecting when DODO_WEBHOOK_SECRET is set but headers are missing.
Not exercised in this run (no active launches). Code path uses x-cron-secret == DODO_WEBHOOK_SECRET (re-uses webhook secret as cron secret per design).

action_items: OPTIONAL: Harden /api/billing/webhook to reject if DODO_WEBHOOK_SECRET is set but any of the standard-webhooks headers (webhook-id/signature/timestamp) are missing — currently it silently skips verification.
OPTIONAL: Add Pydantic Literal/Enum validation for InviteReq.role and CheckoutReq.plan/billing_period to fail fast on bad input.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py line ~527: Webhook signature verification is conditional on all three headers + secret being present. If a malicious caller omits headers, verify is bypassed and event is processed. Should reject when secret is configured but headers missing.

server.py line ~114: get_user_from_token decodes JWT WITHOUT signature verification (verify_signature=False). This is acceptable since Supabase JWT secret is not in env, but every downstream call uses assert_workspace_member to validate authorization, so the unverified sub claim is the authorization key. Spoofing a JWT to claim another user's sub is theoretically possible — recommend adding Supabase JWT secret to env and verifying signature.
server.py: 679 lines in a single file — consider splitting into routers (ai.py, billing.py, team.py, scoring.py) for maintainability.
All AI endpoints correctly enforce workspace membership and quota before calling provider chain — good defensive design.
RLS verified working: a non-member calling Supabase REST directly with their JWT receives [] (empty) for signals/opportunities of the workspace.
test_credentials: Programmatically-created Supabase users via admin API (test+<ts>@astrixai.app / TestPass123!). All cleaned up in fixture teardown.
seed_data_creation: Each test session creates 1 workspace + 4 accounts + 6 signals + 2 problems + 2 opportunities via /api/seed-sample. Workspace + cascade is deleted in teardown. Test users deleted via Supabase admin DELETE.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend is fully functional. Test suite at /app/backend/tests/test_api.py runs all 21 tests in ~65s including 4 real Gemini AI calls and 2 LIVE Dodo checkout creations. Re-run with: pytest /app/backend/tests/ -v. All fixtures self-clean. Dodo LIVE mode confirmed returning real checkout.dodopayments.com URLs. RLS confirmed isolating non-members at Supabase level. AI quota RPC working — initial allowed=true with used/limit/plan fields returned.
rca of the issue: No bugs found. All endpoints in scope returned 200 with correct response shape. Auth gate (401), membership gate (403), and RLS (empty list at REST level) all enforced as designed.
